trajectory_summary: The call moved from warm open to booked appointment efficiently, but the booking carries fragility flags. Carter's early framing of the call as quote-delivery created an expectation gap that Jennifer surfaced at 06:14, and her close-of-call 'think about it for a month or two' signals she has mentally categorized the appointment as low-commitment browsing. Jennifer's rapid compliance and warm close are genuine counter-signals — she is not resistant — but the appointment was not anchored to her bill pain or any curiosity about her specific numbers.
outcome_dispute: The call produced a booked appointment, which is the correct primary outcome tag. However, the booking carries fragility flags: Jennifer's stated intent to 'think about it for a month or two' and the absence of any bill anchor or curiosity creation mean the closer inherits a browsing-posture customer rather than a motivated one. 'Booked with fragility flags' is the accurate read — not a clean sit.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Have the brochures and prices ready, and and I at least can look at them and and and think about it for a month or two till I figure out what's going on.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Jennifer closes in browsing mode and Carter accepts it. The button_up protocol — time anchor, 'no driving / at your computer,' 'is that fair' handshake — exists to convert a booked slot into a felt commitment. Carter skipped all of it. On a call where no bill flip and no curiosity creation occurred, the button_up is the last line of defense against a no-show. Walk Carter through the full sequence and connect each element to show rate.

key_moments.quote: I just have a few questions for you, and then we'll be able to get you a quote for your home.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carter frames the appointment as quote delivery before the verify section is even complete. This is an expectation-framing error, not scope creep — Carter is not explaining solar, but is promising a deliverable (a quote) that belongs to the closer's proposal stage. The error is reactive: no evidence Carter is deliberately setting up a later reframe.

key_moments.quote: is there anyone else on the title of the home with you?
key_moments.stewart_read: Jennifer's answer is ambiguous — she describes a co-title holder but concludes 'I'm the owner' (02:01). Carter accepts this self-identification without confirming whether the other title holder needs to be present for a decision. Per protocols.spouse_decision and protocols.adult_child_decision_maker, anyone on the title is a co-decision-maker who should be included in the appointment. Carter did not probe whether this person would need to be involved.

key_moments.quote: I was just curious because because I needed I needed a quote and to see, you know, what kind of investment you have to put in initially and what type you get back later.
key_moments.stewart_read: Jennifer is articulating the exact ROI framing that makes a solar pitch land — she wants to know cost-in vs. value-back. This is not an objection; it is an invitation. Carter's response ('Yeah. I do. I just yeah. And I'll actually introduce some new programs') is a vague reassurance that neither anchors her framing nor creates curiosity about her specific numbers. The flip available here: her bill pain (even at $50-60/month summer, with a projected winter spike) is the 'investment you're already making' — the closer's design will show her what she gets back. Carter had the raw material and did not use it.

key_moments.quote: And with all the government changes going on, I ain't counting on them at all.
key_moments.stewart_read: Jennifer is expressing skepticism about government incentives — a real and common concern. The correct move here is a clean deferral that also creates curiosity: the specialist handles incentive questions, and the design will show what the numbers look like with or without them. Carter's response ('I'll actually introduce some new programs that will be a guide to go over with you tomorrow') is vague and slightly misleading — it sounds like Carter is promising to personally present programs, when a specialist is coming. The primary coaching frame is deferral-with-poor-execution, not a missed flip. Carter correctly avoided going into incentive detail (scope discipline), but the deferral did not create any curiosity or anchor Jennifer's attention on what the appointment will actually show her.
